calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Order Striking Claim
Off Calendar Sep-14-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING CLAIM IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. THE COURT FINDS THAT ADDITIONAL ORDERS TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY WOULD NOT RESULT IN COMPLAINCE. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT.

Notice Of Motion For Order Striking Claim
Off Calendar Jul-07-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Petitioner's Motion For Order Striking Claim is ordered off calendar as the motion was not timely filed. The motion was not filed until June 15, 2005. C.C.P. Section 1005(b) requires that the motion be filed as well served in a timely fashion. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories;Judicial Council Form Interrogatories; Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents

MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Petitioner's Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner's First Set of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories; and First Set of Demand For Production and Inspection of Documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Claimant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
